commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f140b372307d4ccd75621128a80090bc042c528b)
commands.review_summary:
The branch hardens plugin, provider, Codex app-server, channel, gateway, and agent schema/metadata readers against hostile or unreadable plugin-owned values, with broad fuzz-style regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. Current-base source directly traverses provider tool arrays and plugin/provider fields, and the PR tests use throwing getters and revoked proxies to exercise those failure paths; I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A focused automated repair can address the provider-tools per-index copy blocker; maintainers still own the broader fail-closed policy and proof decision after that repair.

target.title: fix(plugin): harden schema and metadata fuzz boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- hardens plugin-owned schema and metadata boundaries found during fuzzing with synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` fixtures
- latest refresh guards provider install catalog projection: unreadable plugin registry install rows, persisted install records, package-authored install metadata, and revoked proxy rows no longer abort provider setup/reinstall catalog assembly before healthy sibling choices are matched
- branch also covers manifest provider-auth choice projection, setup manifest/descriptor projection, installed plugin index projection, shared capability-provider runtime projection, memory public artifacts, memory prompt projection, preferred-provider live catalog projection, OpenAI strict tool schema inventory projection, trusted-tool-policy projection, agent tool-result middleware discovery, Codex app-server plugin factory discovery, channel setup rows, provider discovery fallbacks beside runtime manifest catalogs, loader cache-key metadata, plugin registry alias reads, provider tool schema arrays, configured model catalog API reads, and recent mainline conflict surfaces
- current head is `f140b372307d4ccd75621128a80090bc042c528b`
- final focused proof ran on `origin/main` at `98a1aa491fadc7cccc47a697936065b38cbb1375`; after push, GitHub reports the PR mergeable against base `98a1aa491fadc7cccc47a697936065b38cbb1375`

## Verification

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/provider-install-catalog.test.ts --reporter=verbose`: 1 file passed, 13 tests passed
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/provider-install-catalog.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "unreadable registry install rows"`: 1 file passed, 1 test passed, 12 skipped before the full-file proof above
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/provider-install-catalog.ts src/plugins/provider-install-catalog.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugins/provider-install-catalog.ts src/plugins/provider-install-catalog.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- branch diff, PR body, and spec scrubbed for the reported plugin name and private local paths: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`: clean for the provider install catalog patch; no code edits after the final clean review
- previous refreshed provider-auth choice proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/provider-auth-choices.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed 1 file, 15 tests
- previous refreshed setup manifest/descriptor proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/setup-registry.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "unreadable setup manifest rows|unreadable setup provider"` passed 1 file, 2 tests, 23 skipped
- previous refreshed installed plugin index proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/installed-plugin-index.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "unreadable synthetic installed plugin rows"` passed 1 file, 1 test, 29 skipped
- previous refreshed capability-provider runtime proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/capability-provider-runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "unreadable active capability provider"` passed 1 file, 2 tests, 47 skipped
- previous refreshed memory public artifact proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/memory-host-core.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed 1 file, 6 tests
- previous refreshed memory prompt proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/memory-host-core.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed 1 file, 5 tests
- previous refreshed preferred-provider catalog proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/flows/model-picker.provider-catalog.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed 1 file, 3 tests
- previous refreshed OpenAI strict-schema proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openai-tool-schema.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "unreadable synthetic tool rows|unreadable synthetic tool schemas"` passed 1 file, 2 tests
- previous refreshed plugin status proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/status.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "skips unreadable inspect registry entries"` passed 1 file, 1 test
- previous refreshed trusted-policy proof: focused `host-hooks.contract.test.ts` and `agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts` slices passed
- previous refreshed agent tool-result middleware proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/agent-tool-result-middleware.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed 1 file, 7 tests
- previous refreshed Codex app-server plugin factory proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/codex-app-server-extension-factory.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed 1 file, 2 tests
- previous refreshed shard proof remains relevant for the broader stack: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/loader.runtime-registry.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts src/agents/schema/clean-for-gemini.test.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry.test.ts src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts src/plugins/provider-discovery.runtime.test.ts src/flows/channel-setup.status.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed 2 Vitest shards; 3 unit-fast files / 51 tests and 4 plugin files / 195 tests
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`: blocked before remote execution because Blacksmith is not authenticated (`blacksmith auth login` required)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: plugin-owned schema, metadata, command metadata, hook registration, tool registration options, CLI registration options, bundled agent tool result middleware options, captured middleware options, captured capability ids, shared capability provider entries, installed plugin index rows, provider install catalog rows, provider install records, package-authored install metadata, setup manifest rows, setup descriptor fields, provider-auth alias maps, manifest provider-auth choice rows, setup auth descriptor rows, provider/runtime/channel/plugin registry projections, model/provider install catalog projections, provider discovery projection, web search install catalog projection, model picker provider catalog projection, preferred-provider live catalog projection, memory prompt projection, memory public artifact projection, web search setup provider projection, provider model-picker flow projection, provider setup flow projection, provider runtime hook-ref projection, channel setup selection projection, configured model catalog projection, OpenAI strict tool schema inventory projection, Codex app-server plugin factory discovery, agent tool-result middleware discovery, and trusted tool policy projection no longer trust crashy getters, throwing plugin builders, revoked proxies, mutable arrays/records, malformed runtime entries, malformed ids, unreadable aliases, unreadable hook aliases, unreadable env vars, unreadable install/provider metadata, unreadable installed-index rows, unreadable setup rows, unreadable setup descriptors, unreadable provider-auth aliases, unreadable provider-auth choices, unreadable setup auth descriptors, unreadable catalog hooks, unreadable live catalog model rows, unreadable public artifact rows, unreadable capability provider rows, unreadable setup provider rows, unreadable setup choices, unreadable channel setup rows, unreadable model-picker contribution rows, unreadable configured model API rows, unreadable strict tool rows, unreadable Codex plugin factory entries, unreadable middleware runtime/handler entries, unreadable policy rows, or unreadable policy ids while healthy sibling registrations continue where the surface is availability-oriented and security-sensitive policy/scope failures fail closed.

Real environment tested: local linked OpenClaw worktree on the draft PR branch, current head `f140b372307d4ccd75621128a80090bc042c528b`, focused-tested on `origin/main` at `98a1aa491fadc7cccc47a697936065b38cbb1375`; after push, GitHub reports the PR mergeable against base `98a1aa491fadc7cccc47a697936065b38cbb1375`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the full provider install catalog Vitest file, ran formatting, targeted linting, commit diff check, private-name scrub, autoreview until clean, and attempted the Blacksmith-backed changed gate listed in Verification.

Evidence after fix: `provider-install-catalog.test.ts` now places unreadable `fuzzplugin` plugin ids, unreadable package install metadata, and revoked proxy rows before a healthy `mockplugin` provider install; catalog listing and choice lookup preserve the healthy provider install metadata instead of throwing. Existing focused coverage still proves provider-auth choice row isolation, setup manifest/descriptor row isolation, installed plugin index row isolation, capability-provider row isolation, memory public artifact row isolation, memory prompt builder isolation, preferred-provider live catalog projection, OpenAI strict-schema diagnostics, inspect-registry projection, trusted policy enforcement, provider discovery, channel setup selection, plugin registry projections, provider tool schema normalization, configured model catalog projection, Codex app-server plugin factory discovery, and middleware discovery.

Observed result after fix: malformed provider install catalog rows no longer abort provider setup/reinstall catalog assembly; malformed provider-auth manifest rows no longer abort provider onboarding/auth-choice resolution; unknown-origin rows do not become trusted workspace choices; explicitly enabled workspace choices are preserved. Malformed setup manifest rows/descriptors no longer abort setup registry/provider/CLI lookup; malformed installed plugin index rows no longer abort cold plugin inventory/enabled checks; malformed capability provider rows no longer abort capability provider resolution; malformed memory public artifact rows no longer abort artifact listing; provider-level artifact failures still reject instead of looking like authoritative empty inventories; malformed memory prompt plugin builders no longer abort prompt/context assembly; malformed live provider catalog model rows no longer crash preferred-provider model picker catalog loading; malformed strict tool inventory rows no longer crash strict-schema diagnostics or strict flag resolution; malformed status inspect registry entries stay isolated; security-sensitive trusted policy unreadability still blocks deterministically instead of failing open.

What was not tested: broad Blacksmith/Testbox changed gate did not run because the local Blacksmith CLI is unauthenticated (`blacksmith auth login` required). AWS Crabbox broad gate was not rerun for this refresh; current proof is the targeted local slice plus formatter/lint/diff/scrub/autoreview checks.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
